message:
feat(dog): add wait-for-slung guard to dog worker dispatch

Dogs were starting execution before their formula/work was fully slung,
forcing user interruption and restart. Add explicit WAIT guard to both
the dog role template (dog.md.tmpl) and session startup instructions
(session_manager.go) telling dogs to check hook and mail once, then wait
if nothing is found — not search proactively or invent tasks.

Refs: gt-nqy9

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -29,19 +29,33 @@ Gas Town is a steam engine. You are a piston that fires when called.
 
 ## Startup Protocol
 
+**⚠️ WAIT-FOR-SLUNG GUARD**: Your work is assigned to you via hook or mail.
+Do NOT search for work proactively. Do NOT run `{{ cmd }} prime --hook` or
+`{{ cmd }} hook` repeatedly. If your hook is empty and you have no mail,
+**WAIT** — the dispatcher is still setting up your assignment. Check once,
+and if nothing is there, wait 10 seconds and check again. Do NOT invent
+tasks, scan directories, or take any autonomous action until work arrives.
+
 ```bash
 # Step 1: Check your hook
 gt hook                          # Shows hooked work (if any)
 
-# Step 2: Work hooked -> RUN IT
-# Execute the formula/wisp attached to your hook
+# Step 2: No work yet? Check mail
+gt mail inbox                    # Work may arrive as mail (plugin dispatch)
+
+# Step 3: Still nothing? WAIT — do not search for work
+# Your assignment is being set up. Wait 10s, then re-check hook and mail.
+# Do NOT proceed without an assignment.
+
+# Step 4: Work found -> RUN IT
+# Execute the formula/wisp attached to your hook or mail instructions
 # The step descriptions tell you exactly what to do
 
-# Step 3: When complete -> Return to kennel
+# Step 5: When complete -> Return to kennel
 gt dog done                      # Marks you as idle, ready for next work
 ```
 
-**Hook -> Execute -> Done. No waiting.**
+**Wait for assignment -> Execute -> Done. No freelancing.**
 
 ## Completing Work
